reasoning: The ECB Deposit Facility Rate currently stands at 2.0%. The most recent Governing Council monetary policy meeting was on October 30, 2025, where rates were maintained at 2.0%. According to Reuters polling from October 2025, all 88 economists expect the ECB to keep rates on a prolonged pause (potentially until 2027) due to steady inflation and weak growth, following an extensive cutting cycle from 4.0% down to 2.0% over the last few years. The next meeting is likely in mid-December (after the Dec 9 target date), making a hike prior to the first two resolution dates near practically impossible as there will be no policy decision. Even looking out to February 2026, market and economist consensus is universally for either a hold or further cuts, with rate hikes effectively priced out or viewed as extremely improbable. Probabilities are set to the lowest bound recommended for extreme unlikelihood, scaling slightly higher over time continuously acknowledging increased background uncertainty.
